title: Traits 3
description: Trait bounds constrain generic types to only accept types that implement certain traits.
instructions: Add the correct trait bound to make the function work with any type that implements Display.
hints: Use trait bounds to accept any type implementing Licensed.
The syntax is: fn func(param: &impl TraitName) or fn func<T: TraitName>(param: &T)
Both parameters need to implement Licensed but can be different types.
explanation: Trait bounds specify that a generic type must implement a particular trait. The `impl Trait` syntax is shorthand for a full generic declaration: `fn func(param: &impl Licensed)` is equivalent to `fn func<T: Licensed>(param: &T)`. This lets the function accept any type that implements Licensed.
